live_call_layer.stack: Matrix-compatible voice/video through the CallChat homeserver, local MatrixRTC authorization, and a self-hosted LiveKit SFU
live_call_layer.transport_security: WebRTC DTLS-SRTP plus Matrix device/account identity controls

q_call_layer.scope: Owner-controlled MatrixRTC, self-hosted LiveKit, Matrix device identity, DTLS-SRTP, TURN, frame encryption, and a required in-memory ZMath room factor
q_call_layer.user_experience: The hosted CallChat Shield client blocks protected calls until ZMath is unlocked and a room-scoped media factor is prepared.

qpu_factor.key_path: A browser-local nonce and validated IonQ hardware result digest are combined with HKDF-SHA-512 into a separate factor required by the QPU-factor ZShield and ZMath profiles.
qpu_factor.classification: Hardware-linked independent encryption factor; not QKD or quantum cryptography

provider_boundary: The browser creates the secret nonce and final QPU factor locally.
IonQ receives only a SHA-256 nonce commitment and the bounded circuit job.
The CallChat service validates and hashes the IonQ result but never receives the nonce or final factor.
No AI or IonQ provider receives a ZMath passphrase, pattern, Matrix key, media key, final QPU factor, or protected plaintext.
